claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to check if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who is 9 years old, and Blake, who is 5. They both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have two children, Alex (age 9) and Blake (age 5), who both live at home with you.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to understand a bit more about your situation.

Are you the person primarily responsible for Alex and Blake's care, or is there someone else (such as another parent or guardian) who is also responsible for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm the one primarily responsible for them - they live with me and I look after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Just to confirm - is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake, such as another parent or guardian?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's the breakdown:

**For Alex (age 9):**
You qualify for Child Benefit because Alex is under 16 years old, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**For Blake (age 5):**
You qualify for Child Benefit because Blake is under 16 years old, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**Important note:** Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim Child Benefit - you can still claim it and receive the payments, but you may need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

Only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, so since no one else is claiming for Alex or Blake, you're free to make your claim for both children.

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria.

Blake is 5 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Blake's care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Blake meets all the eligibility criteria.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is primarily responsible for two children: Alex (age 9) and Blake (age 5). Both children live at home with the claimant, are under 16 years old, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.

Blake meets all the eligibility criteria.
